title: ED referral documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.
red_flags.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC disposition material supports recording measured vital data, manual assessment, current severity and clinician-entered plan conveyed during referral; it does not create destination or urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured

examination.text: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.
examination.rationale: The exact RCEM handover section supports recording clinician-reviewed results, procedures, disposition, specialist discussion and referral or follow-up only when actually completed; it does not decide to refer.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports recording the presenting complaint, investigations or procedures, outcome, follow-up, responsible party, referral and discharge destination in a discharge handover.

exact_section.heading: GP Communication and example discharge-letter headings

sources_used.title: Discharge to General Practice
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency and primary care; requires UAE pathway adaptation

source_limitations: Referral reason, specialty, urgency, named accepting clinician, communication time, advice received, destination, transport, documents sent and contingency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
